pubmed_links: [Kalus et al. (2015) Sulf1 and Sulf2 Differentially Modulate Heparan Sulfate Proteoglycan Sulfation during Postnatal Cerebellum Development](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26448642/); [Joy et al. (2015) Sulf1 and Sulf2 expression in the nervous system and its role in limiting neurite outgrowth in vitro](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25448158/); [Miya et al. (2026) Altered Excitability and Glutamatergic Synaptic Transmission in the Medium Spiny Neurons of the Nucleus Accumbens in Mice Deficient in the Heparan Sulfate Endosulfatase Sulf1](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41381361/)
summary: SULF1 encodes a heparan sulfate 6-O-endosulfatase widely expressed in the nervous system; Sulf1 knockout mice exhibit impaired neurite outgrowth, deficits in cerebellar development, and altered neuronal excitability and glutamatergic synaptic transmission in the nucleus accumbens, implicating SULF1 in reward-aversion circuitry and psychiatric conditions including schizophrenia. (emerging evidence)
